folio.label: 178r
folio.l: No poore Cloistrer , ne no nowys
But a gouänour , wyly and wys
And there w al , of braunes & of bones
A wel faryngö ¹sone , for the nones
I preye to god , ©eue hi~ confusiou~
That first the brougút , vn to Religiou~
Thow woldest han ben , a tredfoul a rigút
Hadestow as gret a leue , as æè hast mygút
To ¹fourme thy lust , in engendrure
Thow haddest bygeten , ful many a creature
Allas , why werest æè , so wyde a cope
God ©eue me sorwe , but and I were Pope
Nat oonly æè , but euäy mygúty man
Thougú he were shore ful heye , vp oon his pan
Shulde haue a wyf , for al the world is lorn~
Religion hatú take vp , al the corn~
Of tredynge , & we borel men ben shrympes
Of feble trees , there come wrecched ympes
This maketú æ oure Eyres , ben so sklendre
And fieble , æ they moun nat wel engendre
This maketú æ oure wyues , woln assaie
Religious folk , for they moun bettre paie
Of venus paiementz , than moun we
God wot , no lussúburgú , payen ©e
But be nat wrotú my lord , thougú æ I pleye
Ful ofte in game , a sootú I haue herd seye
¢ This worthy Monke , toke al in pacience
And seide , I wol doon al my diligence
As fer as sovnetú , in to honestee
To telle ©ow a tale , or two or three
And if ©ow list , to herken hiderward
I wyl ©ow seyn , the lif of Seynt Edward
Or elles first , tgedies wol I telle
Of whicú I haue an .C. , in my celle
Tragedy is to seyn , a certeyn storie
As olde bookes maken , memorie
Of hi~ æ stood , in gret ¸s¹itee
And is I fallen , out of heigú degree
In to Miêie , and endetú wrecchedly
And they ben versified , comunly
Of sexe feet , whicú men clepen exametron
In prose eke , ben endited many oon
And eke in metre , in many a sondry wyse
Loo this declaryngö , ougút I now suffise
